column1: # pstop OTA Partition Table — ESP32-S3 8MB Flash (no factory)
# NVS grown 0x6000 -> 0x10000 (24 KB -> 64 KB) on 2026-07-21 to hold the
# 128-peer boot cache (ml_peer_nvs
# (up to ~14 KB) + settings + wifi list + dcs counters + WG keys with
# headroom. The extra 40 KB comes ENTIRELY from the 56 KB alignment gap
# that used to sit between phy_init and the 0x10000-aligned ota_0 — so:
#   SIZES ARE UNCHANGED. The OTA rollback geometry is byte-identical to
#   the pre-2026-07-21 table.
# Only otadata and phy_init shift up (still before ota_0). Consequences:
#   - A full (cabled) flash of this table is required to activate the
#     larger NVS; it wipes NVS once (re-provision admin settings / peer).
#   - Fielded units on the OLD table are NOT broken by OTA of firmware
#     built against this CSV: OTA flashes only the app image to the
#     inactive ota slot
#     locates nvs/otadata at runtime via the partition API — so an old
#     unit keeps its 24 KB NVS and its (identical) ota_0/ota_1/coredump
#     offsets. The 64 KB NVS activates on the next cabled reflash.
# Name
nvs
otadata
phy_init
ota_0
ota_1
coredump
